quotes.text: People ask why I still tour and I genuinely don't have a clean answer. Some of it's the obvious stuff. But some of it is that I still get nervous before I walk out, and I've decided that's the part I don't ever want to fix.
quotes.speaker: Bruce Springsteen
quotes.context: The New Yorker Radio Hour, extended interview with David Remnick, aired October 2022
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Bruce has talked openly about stage fright and what live performance means to him throughout his career. The tell is that it's slightly too composed; real Bruce in conversation tends to loop back and contradict himself a bit more before landing.

quotes.text: The guitar was made by God
quotes.speaker: Father Dionysios Tabakis
quotes.context: Greek priest and doom metal musician, interviewed in his flat in Nafplio, Greece

source.title: Music — ‘Techno in a monastery – are you ready?’ The Greek priest whose doom metal album

quotes.text: I don't think I've ever written a song in the morning in my life. I've tried. It's not happening. Something about mornings feels like a negotiation I'm not ready to have.
quotes.speaker: Lana Del Rey
quotes.context: Interview Magazine, cover story by Alex Needham, published in the Spring 2023 issue
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Lana rarely discusses process in practical terms, so when she does it tends to be a little oblique and wry, which I leaned into. The giveaway is 'a negotiation I'm not ready to have' — it's the exact kind of line she might actually say, but it's a hair too epigrammatic for pure candid speech.

quotes.text: You know what's wild is that I can't listen to that record anymore. Not because I don't like it — I think it holds up fine — but because when I put it on I don't hear the music, I just hear every room I was in while we were making it. The couch. The takeout containers. All of it.
quotes.speaker: Frank Ocean
quotes.context: i-D Magazine, rare print interview conducted during a pop-up event at Blonded Radio's physical installation in Los Angeles, 2023
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Frank has talked about the intensely personal and spatial memories attached to Blonde's creation, and this kind of sensory-memory observation is very consistent with his public voice. The giveaway is it's a degree too communicative and complete for Frank, who in real interviews tends to leave more unsaid.

quotes.text: I used to get so annoyed when journalists would call something we did 'experimental.' Like, no — we just hadn't figured out the right way to do it yet. Experimental implies you planned the confusion, and we absolutely did not.
quotes.speaker: Thom Yorke
quotes.context: The Guardian, long-read profile by Alexis Petridis, published alongside the WALL OF EYES announcement, September 2023
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Thom has a well-documented prickliness about how Radiohead's process gets described by critics, and the distinction he draws here is exactly the kind of pedantic-but-genuine correction he'd make. The tell is that it's slightly too witty and resolved; he usually trails off or qualifies more.

quotes.text: 'Bull riding and boobies' — that is potentially the greatest lyric in any song ever.
quotes.speaker: Harry Styles
quotes.context: In his 2019 Rolling Stone cover interview, riffing on a line from Lil Nas X's 'Old Town Road.'

source.title: Rolling Stone — The Eternal Sunshine of Harry Styles
